conversation_history.answer: Nah, ain’t got no job right now.
conversation_history.question: Thank you for sharing so far. Are you currently employed?

conversation_history.answer: Uh… yeah, I wanna work. Gotta find somethin’, keep busy. Doesn’t gotta be fancy.
conversation_history.question: Are you planning to work after your release?

conversation_history.answer: Yeah, did some day laborin’. Landscaping, movin’ stuff. Ain’t no steady gig.
conversation_history.question: Before entering this facility, were you working a job?

conversation_history.answer: Uh… prob’ly labor work. Landscapin, movin’ boxes, cleanin’. Maybe kitchen help. Anything steady that pays.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing that. What kind of work are you hoping to do after you get out?

conversation_history.answer: Uh… shoot, I’d aim for full-time. Like 35–40 hours a week.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing that. How many hours per week are you hoping to work after release?

conversation_history.answer: Uh… prolly go back day labor at first. If I see somethin’ steady new—like kitchen help—I’ll try that.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing that. Do you plan to go back to the day labor work you did before entering the facility, or will you look for something new after release?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… just steady pay.  
Save enough for my own place.  
Maybe get a car.  
And see my kids more.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing your plans. Do you have any specific goals or dreams for the work you’re looking for after release?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… maybe GED if I can.  
Don’t really think ‘bout college.  
Might try a cookin’ class if they got one.
conversation_history.question: Are you planning to go to school or take any classes after your release?

conversation_history.answer: Uh… prolly like 3 hours GED, 5 hours cookin’. Around 8 hrs a week.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for thinking about classes. How many hours each week are you hoping to spend on GED studies or cooking classes after your release?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… I’ll hit the county adult school for GED.  
Cookin’ class at the community center kitchen.
conversation_history.question: What type of school or program are you planning to attend for your GED studies and cooking classes after your release?

conversation_history.answer: Uh… some high school. Left in 10th grade.
conversation_history.question: Thank you for telling me about your GED and cooking classes plans. What is the highest level of education you have completed? (For example: some high school, high school diploma, GED, or college)

conversation_history.answer: Uh… nope. Ain’t got no certs or nothin’.
conversation_history.question: Do you have any degrees or certificates, like a GED, high school diploma, or other certification?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… wanna pass GED so I can get a decent job.  

Cookin’ class… hope I learn real skills, maybe work at a diner.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing your plans. Do you have any specific goals or hopes for your GED studies or cooking classes?

conversation_history.answer: Nah. Ain’t signed up yet. Might look into the GED class soon.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing your education goals. Are you currently enrolled in any classes or programs here in the facility?

conversation_history.answer: Uh… yeah. Gonna apply for food stamps. Maybe unemployment too.
conversation_history.question: Do you plan to apply for any financial assistance after your release, like food stamps or unemployment benefits?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… don’t wanna be broke all the time.  
Get a job, save some cash.  
Pay my own rent.  
Have lil’ emergency fund so I don’t freak out.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing that. Do you have any specific goals for your finances after release?

conversation_history.answer: Uh… no. Ain’t got nobody.
conversation_history.question: Do you currently have a partner, spouse, or long-term partner?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… not really.  
Ma’s pissed I been inside.  
Pop… don’t say much.  
We cool-ish? Maybe.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing. Do you have a strong, positive relationship with your parents?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… got a sis. She’s cool but busy.  
Cousin Joe checks in sometimes.  
That’s about it.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing about your parents. How about other family members, like siblings or relatives—do you have strong, positive relationships with them?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… I was couch-surfin’.  
Here and there at a buddy’s place.  
Sometimes crashed in a motel.
conversation_history.question: Before entering this facility, where were you living?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… not really.  
Might crash at my buddy Mike’s if he’s cool.  
No spot locked down yet.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing where you stayed before. Do you have a place lined up to stay right after you get out?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… still figuring.  
Prob gotta keep couch-surfin’ or shelter.  
Wish I had my own place, but cash’s tight.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing that. Looking further ahead, where do you plan to live long-term after release? Will it be Mike’s place, back to couch-surfing, or somewhere else?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… wanna snag my own spot.  
No couch-surfin’.  
Cheap room or studio.  
Save up.  
Get my kids over.
conversation_history.question: What are your goals for housing once you’re out?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… I used to shoot hoops at the park.  
Play video games.  
Catch a movie now and then.  
Go fishin’ when I could.
conversation_history.question: Before you came here, what things did you like to do for fun?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… I hoop in the rec yard.  
Watch TV sometimes.  
Play cards with guys.  
Do puzzles if I’m bored.  
Write letters home… that’s it.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing what you liked to do before. While you’re here in the facility, what activities or hobbies do you enjoy?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… hoopin’ for sure.  
Go fishin’ by the lake.  
Hit the gym, lift some.  
Video games now and then.  
Maybe hang with my kids.
conversation_history.question: Thank you for sharing what you do while you’re here. What hobbies or activities would you like to pursue after your release?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… ain’t got a big crew.  
Just my buddy Mike from the program.  
My mom sometimes.  
Maybe my sis.
conversation_history.question: Hi Jordan, before your release, do you have any close friends or companions you plan to spend time with?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… I don’t know.  
Keep it small.  
Hang with Mike, my sis, my mom.  
Maybe meet folks at gym.  
Stay busy so I stay outta trouble.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing that. How do you imagine your social life will look after you’re released?

conversation_history.answer:
Uh… yeah.  
Need NA meetings.  
Maybe a sponsor.  
Therapy check-ins.  
My mom’s watchin’ too.
conversation_history.question: Thanks for sharing so far. Do you think you'll need any support or resources for alcohol or drug use after you’re released?
